chapter: The Making of the National Movement: 1870s–1947

questions.question: What is nationalism?
questions.answer: A feeling of unity and loyalty towards one's nation.

questions.question: In which year was the Indian National Congress formed?
Who helped organize the Indian National Congress?
Who were the Moderates?
questions.answer: Early Congress leaders who used peaceful methods to demand reforms.

questions.question: Name one moderate leader.
What is the Drain of Wealth theory?
questions.answer: A theory explaining how India's wealth was transferred to Britain.

questions.question: Who proposed the Drain of Wealth theory?
In which year was Bengal partitioned?
What was the Swadeshi Movement?
questions.answer: A movement to boycott foreign goods and promote Indian goods.

questions.question: What does Swaraj mean?
Who said 'Swaraj is my birthright and I shall have it'?
Who introduced Satyagraha?
What is Satyagraha?
questions.answer: A method of nonviolent protest based on truth.

questions.question: What was the Non-Cooperation Movement?
questions.answer: A movement encouraging people to boycott British institutions.

questions.question: Which event led to the withdrawal of the Non-Cooperation Movement?
What was the Civil Disobedience Movement?
questions.answer: A movement in which people broke colonial laws peacefully.

questions.question: What was the Salt March?
questions.answer: A protest march against the British salt tax.

questions.question: In which year did the Quit India Movement begin?
What slogan was given during the Quit India Movement?
Which world event influenced the Quit India Movement?
In which year did India gain independence?
Which two countries were formed after partition?
What was the main aim of the national movement?
questions.answer: To achieve independence from British rule.

questions.question: Why was unity important in the freedom struggle?
questions.answer: Unity strengthened the struggle against British rule.

questions.question: Name one important Gandhian movement.
